factors.summary: The company is not facing significant litigation or material investigations that would impact its financial or operational performance.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to our risk factors contained in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the period ended December 31, 2021.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No significant litigation or material investigations impacting financial or operational performance.

factors.summary: Overall demand is stable with strong demand in most markets offset by supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Throughout the first quarter of 2022, we continue to experience strong demand for our products and services in most of the markets we serve. However, the supply chain disruptions and delays, shortages in materials... negatively impact our ability to meet this robust demand.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong demand in most markets despite supply chain issues.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing due to supply chain disruptions and increased demand.

evidence.text: These challenges have also created operational and logistical inefficiencies, which have led to periodic production interruptions and elevated levels of inventory, negatively impacting our productivity, margin performance, working capital and cash flows.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in backlog due to supply chain disruptions and increased demand.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, which have been partially effective in offsetting inflation.

evidence.text: As discussed above, in response to the increased inflationary environment, we continue to implement various pricing initiatives across our global businesses, which drove the overall increase in Net revenues compared to the same period in 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices to offset inflation.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products in Allegion Americas and stable in Allegion International.

evidence.text: Net revenues from non-residential products... driven by improved pricing and higher volumes. In spite of this increase, our non-residential businesses continue to experience... supply chain challenges... Non-residential businesses continue to experience strong demand... residential products... decrease... due to lower volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue shifting towards higher-margin products.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable with slight increases in Allegion Americas and no change in Allegion International.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2022, increased by 4.2%, or $29.3 million, compared with the same period in 2021... Allegion Americas: 5.9% increase... Allegion International: no change.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable organic revenue growth with slight increases.

factors.summary: Volumes are growing in non-residential markets but declining in residential markets due to supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Net revenues from non-residential products... driven by improved pricing and higher volumes. In spite of this increase... residential products... decrease... due to lower volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Volumes growing in non-residential markets.

factors.summary: Non-residential markets are growing faster than residential markets, driven by strong demand and pricing.

evidence.text: Net revenues from non-residential products... driven by improved pricing and higher volumes. In spite of this increase... residential products... decrease... due to lower volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Non-residential markets growing faster due to strong demand.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing and creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation also continues to impact margin performance.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing and creating margin pressure, indicating significant financial strain.

factors.summary: Cost actions include re-engineering product designs and developing alternate sources of supply to mitigate supply chain issues.

evidence.text: We are implementing measures to mitigate operational and distribution inefficiencies, such as re-engineering product designs and configurations to accept alternate electronic components and developing alternate sources of supply.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost actions are being taken to mitigate supply chain issues, but the evidence doesn't indicate success or failure.

factors.summary: Productivity is under pressure due to supply chain disruptions and labor shortages.

evidence.text: Inflation in excess of pricing and productivity reflects the impacts of increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation, as well as the ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, shortages in materials and labor shortages affecting critical suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is under pressure due to supply chain disruptions and labor shortages, which are moderate challenges.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are elevated due to supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: These challenges have also created operational and logistical inefficiencies, which have led to periodic production interruptions and elevated levels of inventory, negatively impacting our productivity, margin performance, working capital and cash flows.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Elevated inventory levels due to supply chain disruptions suggest operational inefficiencies and financial strain.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance despite challenges, expecting continued dynamic macroeconomic conditions.

evidence.text: While we anticipate that fiscal year 2022 will continue to be a dynamic macroeconomic environment, and that each of the above noted factors will continue to impact our global businesses, we are rapidly adapting to navigate these challenges.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is maintaining forward guidance despite challenges, but there's no indication of improvement or deterioration.

factors.summary: Management is cautiously confident, investing in initiatives to mitigate risks and drive growth.

evidence.text: We remain focused on providing exceptional service to our customers; implementing measures to mitigate operational and distribution inefficiencies... and investing in business initiatives to drive future growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is investing in initiatives to mitigate risks and drive growth, showing cautious optimism.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing supply chain issues, inflation, and geopolitical instability.

evidence.text: The on-going COVID-19 pandemic, the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the macroeconomic challenges noted above will likely continue to affect us in numerous and evolving ways.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include significant challenges like inflation and geopolitical instability, indicating clear threats.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on adapting to challenges and investing in future growth initiatives.

evidence.text: We have also realized pricing improvements across all of our major businesses during the first quarter of 2022 as a result of recent pricing initiatives to address inflationary pressures. We will continue to explore various options to control costs and enhance financial performance, while minimizing disruption to customers and the overall business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Long-term strategy focuses on adapting to challenges and investing in growth, showing proactive planning.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include inflation, supply chain stability, and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: Increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation also continues to impact margin performance. The on-going COVID-19 pandemic, the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the macroeconomic challenges noted above will likely continue to affect us in numerous and evolving ways.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased inflation, supply chain instability, and geopolitical tensions pose significant risks to margin performance.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks include the Russian invasion of Ukraine, impacting supply chain and creating instability.

evidence.text: The recent invasion of Ukraine by Russia has resulted in regional instability and the introduction of heightened economic sanctions against Russia by the U.S. and the international community. Although our presence in both countries is limited... monitoring these developments and the potential economic and financial consequences as the situation evolves.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The Russian invasion of Ukraine introduces instability and economic sanctions, impacting supply chains despite limited direct exposure.

factors.summary: Market share is stable with no clear indication of gain or loss.

evidence.text: Throughout the first quarter of 2022, we continue to experience strong demand for our products and services in most of the markets we serve... No specific mention of market share changes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific mention of market share changes, indicating stability.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are stable with no major changes mentioned.

evidence.text: Throughout the first quarter of 2022, we continue to experience strong demand for our products and services in most of the markets we serve... No specific mention of competitive dynamics changes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No major changes noted in competitive dynamics, suggesting stability.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is stable with no specific changes mentioned.

evidence.text: Throughout the first quarter of 2022, we continue to experience strong demand for our products and services in most of the markets we serve... No specific mention of customer concentration changes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific changes mentioned in customer concentration, indicating stability.

factors.summary: CapEx is increasing due to investments in operational efficiency and growth initiatives.

evidence.text: We are rapidly adapting to navigate these challenges... investing in business initiatives to drive future growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures indicate investment in growth and operational efficiency.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is decreasing due to lower net earnings and changes in working capital.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities during the three months ended March 31, 2022, decreased $91.3 million compared to the same period in 2021, primarily driven by decreased Net earnings and changes in working capital.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to lower net earnings and working capital changes suggests financial strain.

factors.summary: Interest rate impact is minimal with a slight decrease in interest expense due to lower rates.

evidence.text: Interest expense for the three months ended March 31, 2022, decreased $0.4 million compared with the same period in 2021, primarily due to a lower weighted-average interest rate on our outstanding indebtedness.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Minimal impact from interest rate changes, with a slight decrease in expense, indicating stability.

factors.summary: FX exposure is creating margin compression due to unfavorable exchange rates.

evidence.text: Currency exchange rates (1.9) % Total 4.2 %... unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements... Currency exchange rates (6.8) % Total — %.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates that FX exposure is causing margin compression due to unfavorable exchange rates.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing significant increases in input costs, driven by inflation in commodities, materials, and labor, as well as rising freight costs. These cost increases are negatively impacting the company's financial performance and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: adverse impacts to our business operations due to the global COVID-19 pandemic, supply chain constraints, electronic component and labor shortages, inflation, rising freight and material costs
Increased commodity, material component, packaging, freight and labor inflation also continues to impact margin performance.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is facing significant increases in input costs driven by inflation and supply chain issues, negatively impacting financial performance.

factors.summary: The company is facing margin compression due to inflation and other cost pressures, which are not fully offset by pricing actions. Challenges include rising input and labor costs, supply chain disruptions, and increased cyber-attack risks.

evidence.text: The company's margins are under pressure due to rising input and labor costs, which are not fully offset by pricing actions.

inflation, rising freight and material costs, impacts of Russia's invasion of Ukraine, including further supply chain disruptions and the increased risk of cyber-attacks in connection with such invasion
The decreases in Operating income and Operating margin were primarily due to inflation and productivity challenges in excess of pricing improvements.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is experiencing margin compression due to inflation and cost pressures, which are not fully offset by pricing actions.

factors.summary: The company's debt levels are relatively stable, showing a slight decrease in total debt from $1,442.1 million to $1,439.4 million. Additionally, the liquidity outlook highlights strong operating cash flows and low leverage, indicating a stable financial position. However, there are no specific changes mentioned in the liquidity section regarding debt leverage, which suggests a continuation of the current stance on debt management. Overall, the debt leverage appears to be stable with a minor decrease in total debt, supporting a positive assessment of the company's financial health in terms of debt management.

evidence.text: "Total borrowings outstanding 1,447.2 1,450.3 Discounts and debt issuance costs, net (7.8) (8.2) Total debt 1,439.4 1,442.1 Less current portion of long-term debt 12.6 12.6 Total long-term debt $ 1,426.8 $ 1,429.5"

"Our primary source of liquidity is cash provided by operating activities... Unused availability under our 2021 Revolving Facility... Strong operating cash flows, low leverage and low capital intensity"

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's debt levels are stable with a slight decrease, strong operating cash flows, and low leverage, indicating a stable financial position.

factors.summary: The supply chain health is facing significant challenges, including disruptions, delays, material shortages, and labor shortages, which are impacting the company's operations and its ability to meet strong demand.

evidence.text: adverse impacts to our business operations due to the global COVID-19 pandemic, supply chain constraints, electronic component and labor shortages

Throughout the first quarter of 2022, we continue to experience strong demand for our products and services in most of the markets we serve. However, the supply chain disruptions and delays, shortages in materials... negatively impact our ability to meet this robust demand.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The supply chain is facing significant challenges including disruptions, delays, and shortages, impacting operations and demand fulfillment.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated a commitment to returning value to shareholders through both consistent dividend payments and active share repurchase programs. The evidence highlights significant share repurchases, with over 533,000 shares purchased in the first quarter alone, and dividend payments totaling $0.41 per ordinary share. These actions indicate a positive approach to enhancing shareholder returns.

evidence.text: Period Total number of shares purchased (000s) January 1 - January 31 — $ — — $ 201,435 February 1 - February 28 190 113.09 190 179,941 March 1 - March 31 343 114.98 343 140,454 Total 533 $ 114.31 533 $ 140,454
During the three months ended March 31, 2022, we paid dividends of $0.41 per ordinary share to shareholders and repurchased approximately 0.5 million shares for $61.0 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively returning value to shareholders through consistent dividends and significant share repurchases.

factors.summary: The economic conditions are challenging, characterized by inflation, supply chain issues, geopolitical instability, and the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and the Russian invasion of Ukraine. These factors are acting as significant headwinds for the company.

evidence.text: economic, political and business conditions in the markets in which we operate, including changes to trade agreements, sanctions, import and export regulations and custom duties

While we anticipate that fiscal year 2022 will continue to be a dynamic macroeconomic environment... increased inflationary pressures... The on-going COVID-19 pandemic, the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the macroeconomic challenges noted above will likely continue to affect us in numerous and evolving ways.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are challenging with inflation, supply chain issues, geopolitical instability, and the ongoing impacts of the pandemic and Russian invasion.

factors.summary: The company has engaged in significant merger and acquisition (MA) activity, notably the acquisition of Access Technologies, which is expected to be accretive. The transaction is planned to be financed through a combination of cash, borrowings under the 2021 Revolving Facility, and new debt financing.

evidence.text: As discussed in Note 20 to the Condensed and Consolidated Financial Statements, on April 22, 2022, we signed a definitive agreement to acquire Stanley Access Technologies LLC... We intend to finance this transaction through a combination of cash, borrowings under our 2021 Revolving Facility and new debt financing.

As discussed in Note 20 to the Condensed and Consolidated Financial Statements, on April 22, 2022, we signed a definitive agreement to acquire Stanley Access Technologies LLC... We intend to finance this transaction through a combination of cash, borrowings under our 2021 Revolving Facility and new debt financing.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While the company is engaging in MA activity, the evidence does not clearly indicate whether it will be accretive or not.
